name: Barrancas de Burujón Circular Trail
name_original: Monumento Natural Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña – trail run circular por las Barrancas de Torralba-Burujón
description: Run a moderate 4.8-mile loop through the "Spanish Grand Canyon" at Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña, with dramatic views.
region_intro: Jogging around Burujón offers routes through a landscape characterized by dramatic clay and sandstone cliffs, known as the Barrancas, which rise nearly 100 meters above the Castrejón reservoir and the Tagus River. This region features rugged terrain shaped by erosion, providing varied elevations and scenic viewpoints. The area's natural features create a distinct environment for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Burujón The most popular jogging route is Barrancas de Burujón Circular Trail, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 58 minutes to complete. This route gently curves around the cliffs, offering different perspectives of the landscape and the Castrejón Lake. Another top favourite among local runners is Torrijos Town Center loop from Torrijos, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This route explores the local urban and suburban areas of Torrijos. Local runners also love the Running loop from Torrijos, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through local landscapes, often completed in about 45 minutes. Jogging around Burujón is defined by dramatic clay and sandstone cliffs, the Castrejón reservoir, and the Tagus River. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Burujón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Burujón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Burujón?
A: Burujón offers a diverse network of over 20 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These trails allow you to explore the unique landscape of the Barrancas and the surrounding areas.

Q: Are there easy running routes in Burujón suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, Burujón features several easy running routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambrón Viewpoint – Enebros Viewpoint loop from Río Las Cabreras&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.6 km trail, perfect for a relaxed jog. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambrón Viewpoint – Enebros Viewpoint loop from Estacionamiento PMR «Mirador de los Enebros»&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.5 km and also rated easy.

Q: What kind of natural features and viewpoints can I expect to see while jogging in Burujón?
A: Jogging in Burujón offers breathtaking views of the dramatic clay and sandstone cliffs, known as the Barrancas, which rise nearly 100 meters above the Castrejón reservoir and the Tagus River. You can visit impressive viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Cambrón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enebros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provide privileged perspectives of the entire ravine and the lake. The trails often wind along the water, offering stunning reflections of the cliffs.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Burujón?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular choice in Burujón. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021191768/barrancas-de-burujon-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrancas de Burujón Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a highly recommended 7.7 km loop that offers a comprehensive experience of the area, gently curving around the cliffs and providing different perspectives of the landscape and Castrejón Lake.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Burujón?
A: The best times to go running in Burujón are during spring and autumn. Temperatures are milder and the light is optimal for photography and longer runs. The contrast of the sun-baked, red earth with the blue water of the lake is particularly stunning during these seasons.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Burujón?
A: While the guide doesn't explicitly state dog-friendly policies for every trail, many natural areas in Spain permit dogs on trails, especially if kept on a leash. Given the natural monument status, it's generally advisable to keep dogs leashed to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Always check local signage for specific restrictions.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options in Burujón?
A: Many of the easier trails in Burujón, such as the shorter loops around the viewpoints, are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambrón Viewpoint – Enebros Viewpoint loop from Río Las Cabreras&lt;/a&gt;, at 4.6 km and rated easy, could be a good option for families looking for a scenic and manageable run or walk together.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the running trails in Burujón?
A: There is a dedicated parking area for visitors to the Barrancas de Burujón. You can find it at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parking area at Las Barrancas de Burujón&lt;/a&gt;, which provides convenient access to the main trails and viewpoints.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Burujón?
A: The running routes in Burujón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the dramatic scenery, the unique geological formations of the Barrancas, and the stunning views over the Castrejón reservoir. The variety of trails, from easy loops to more moderate distances, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting while running in Burujón?
A: Yes, the Barrancas de Burujón is a haven for birdwatching. While jogging, you might spot various bird species, including golden eagles, griffon vultures, and barn swallows soaring overhead. The quiet refuge offers a chance to observe wildlife, adding another dimension to your outdoor experience.

Q: Can I find any cafes or places to eat near the running routes in Burujón?
A: While the immediate area around the Barrancas de Burujón is primarily natural, the nearby town of Torrijos offers amenities such as cafes and restaurants. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijos Town Center loop from Torrijos&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Torrijos&lt;/a&gt;, you'll find options within the town itself.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes in the Burujón area?
A: While many routes are moderate, some offer longer distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijos Town Center loop from Torrijos&lt;/a&gt; is a 10 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021191768/barrancas-de-burujon-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrancas de Burujón Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is 7.7 km, both providing a good workout with moderate difficulty. The varied terrain and elevations in the region ensure a satisfying challenge for those seeking it.
tips: One of the viewpoints located at the southern end of the ravine path, with a privileged view of the entire ravine, its curious natural formation and the Castrejón reservoir, a whole dream complex.

This wonderful place, declared a Natural Monument, is worth visiting and exploring it completely. With 4 viewpoints connected by a path, you will have beautiful views of the Castrejón reservoir and the Tagus river in each of them, all deserving of a postcard!

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for jogging this circular trail?
faqs.answer: The komoot tour data suggests an average jogging duration of approximately 58 minutes for this 7.74 km circular route. However, this can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views. For hiking, similar routes are estimated to take 2-3 hours.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner trail runners?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally rated as moderate. While many paths are easily accessible, some sections, particularly those near cliff edges, require good fitness and sure-footedness due to the fragile terrain. It's more accurately described as a scenic trail suitable for trail running that may involve some walking in technical sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Barrancas de Torralba-Burujón trail?
faqs.answer: The landscape features spectacular clayey cuts formed by erosion. You'll encounter easily accessible paths and dusty dirt tracks. Be aware that some sections, especially those skirting the cliff edges, may be unprotected and require caution due to the fragile ground.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers breathtaking panoramic views of the reddish and orange clay cliffs, which can reach up to 120 meters. Key viewpoints include the Enebros Viewpoint and the View of the Barrancas de Burujón and the Castrejón Reservoir from Mirador del Cambrón. These spots provide impressive photographic opportunities, especially when the sun illuminates the cliffs in the afternoon.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Natural del Tajo, the Sendero de la Barranca, and the Camino Natural del Tajo - GR 113.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit for a trail run?
faqs.answer: Given the significant lack of shade along the entire route, the cooler months of spring and autumn are ideal for visiting. If you go during warmer periods, it's highly recommended to start early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Is the Monumento Natural Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area is generally dog-friendly. However, caution is advised, especially near unprotected cliff edges, to ensure the safety of your pet. Always keep dogs on a leash and be mindful of other visitors and wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Monumento Natural?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Monumento Natural Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point for this specific komoot tour isn't detailed, the Barrancas de Burujón area typically has designated parking areas near popular access points. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot's starting point for the most convenient parking.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a trail run on this route?
faqs.answer: Due to the lack of shade and potentially fragile terrain, essential items include plenty of water, sunscreen, a hat, and appropriate trail running shoes for dusty and uneven surfaces. Binoculars can enhance your wildlife viewing experience.

faqs.question: What makes this trail unique for jogging?
faqs.answer: This trail offers an 'otherworldly' and 'unreal' landscape, often likened to a 'Spanish Grand Canyon,' with its dramatic reddish clay cliffs overlooking the Castrejón reservoir and Tagus River. It provides a visually spectacular and peaceful backdrop for a run, allowing you to immerse yourself in nature's sounds.

waypoints.name: View of the Barrancas de Burujón and the Castrejón Reservoir from Mirador del Cambrón
